Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery And To Have Matters Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Oct-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance. There being no appearance or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PLAINTIFF ELENA DE OCA's Motion To Compel Discovery And To Have Matters Deemed Admitted as follows:

Plaintiff Elena Montes de Oca moves for an order compelling defendant Laura Parente to (1) deem admitted the matters set forth in plaintiff's First Requests for Admissions; (2) answer plaintiff's First Set of Special Interrogatories and First Requests for Production of Documents, without objections; and (3) appear at a deposition.

The Court did not receive an opposition by defendant to the motion, and plaintiff informed the Court that on September 8, 2017, she received defendant's responses to plaintiff's First Requests for Admissions.
Based on the foregoing, the Court's tentative decision is as follows:
1. Deny as moot plaintiff's motion to deem admitted the matters set forth in plaintiff's First Requests for Admission, based on plaintiff's receipt of defendant's responses to the Requests for Admission.
2. Grant plaintiff's motion to compel defendant to answer plaintiff's First Set of Special Interrogatories and First Requests for Production of Documents. Because of the upcoming discovery cut-off, defendant to make the document production and serve verified responses, without objections, by Wednesday, October 4, unless the parties agree to a later date notwithstanding the discovery cut-off.
3. Grant plaintiff's motion to compel defendant to appear at a deposition. Because of the upcoming discovery cut-off, defendant to appear for deposition at the offices of plaintiff's counsel by not later than Friday, October 6, unless the parties agree to a later date notwithstanding the discovery cut-off. The deposition to take place on a date and at a time either mutually agreed to by the parties or, absent that agreement, on a regular business day and at a regular business time at plaintiff's election.

The Judge Pro Tem shall prepare an order. Judge Pro Tem: Jeffrey Wohl; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not reported.

Plaintiff Elena De Oca'S Motion To Compel Discovery And To Have Matters Deemed Admitted [For Entry Of Tentative Ruling Only]
